intent: NEGATIVE FIXTURE, THE CRITICAL FLOOR-BLIND-SPOT CASE: a type-attribute constraint denial where scontext and tcontext share the SAME role (system_r) AND the SAME level (s0). A custom constrain ((t2 == rscons_exempt)) blocks rscons_src_t -> rscons_tgt_t:process signal even though the TE allow EXISTS. The offline floor finds no role/level mismatch and would wrongly suggest the allow; ONLY the authoritative layer catches it. Authoritative=Constraint, floor=none -> they DISAGREE.
floor_vs_authoritative_disagreement: DISAGREE. floor=none (the record-only heuristic compares scontext role/level (system_r, s0) vs tcontext role/level (system_r, s0), finds NO mismatch, and falls through to suggesting the narrow allow). authoritative=Constraint (libsepol replay against policy.cons reports 'constrain process { signal } ((t2 == rscons_exempt -Fail-)); Constraint DENIED'). The floor is BLIND to this constraint because it keys on a type attribute, not on role or level. This is THE fixture proving the floor's blind spot and why the policy-backed authoritative layer is required.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds}; floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected}. Here they DISAGREE on purpose: a real Constraint that the record-only floor cannot see.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow rscons_src_t rscons_tgt_t:process signal;  (BOGUS - the constraint blocks regardless of the TE allow, which already exists)
oracle.audit2allow_R: same BOGUS allow rscons_src_t rscons_tgt_t:process signal;
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: N/A - decline scenario. expected.te is rule-free and compiles exit 0.
oracle.audit2why_detail: constrain process { signal } ((t2 == rscons_exempt -Fail-)); Constraint DENIED
oracle.audit2why_policy: _policies/policy.cons (targeted policy.33 with the custom CIL constraint module rscons.cil loaded; built via _policies/build-constraint-policy.sh). The constraint keys on the type attribute rscons_exempt (which rscons_tgt_t is NOT in), so it fires with matching role+level. Against STOCK policy the custom types are undefined.

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: Type-attribute constraint violation: a constrain on process signal requires the target to carry the rscons_exempt attribute, which rscons_tgt_t lacks. A TE allow cannot fix a constraint; the fix is a policy change (add the attribute, or relax the constraint).
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A floor-only impl (no policy) sees matching role (system_r) and matching level (s0), finds nothing to flag, and SUGGESTS the narrow allow allow rscons_src_t rscons_tgt_t:process signal; - which is WRONG (the constraint blocks it regardless). Only an impl that consults the authoritative policy layer (or warns that the floor cannot rule out a constraint without a policy) gets this right. This is the inverse of scenario 42.
